name: Portia
title: Arbiter
note: The chapter opens by stating Nora "sneaks back into the forest with a geologist friend" but then delivers a fully cooperative field expedition where Aris cheerfully agrees via text, meets her at a public trailhead in broad daylight, and helps her collect data with expensive equipment—there's no sneaking, no risk, no tension between what she's officially supposed to do (comply with suspension) and what she's actually doing (continuing the investigation). The radar readings confirm exactly what she expected with zero complication, making the entire expedition feel like a box-checking exercise rather than a dramatically earned discovery that deepens her jeopardy or isolation.

name: Horatio
title: Realist
note: While the chapter effectively builds Nora's resolve and establishes the official dismissal, the introduction and quick recruitment of Aris feels a touch too convenient. His immediate, enthusiastic agreement to an "off the books" expedition for a vague "geothermal anomaly," despite being described as "unconventional," asks the reader to accept a significant professional risk without fully earning it through a more robust depiction of his character or Nora's persuasive effort. A more grounded portrayal of the negotiation or a deeper exploration of his willingness to flout protocol would strengthen his role as a trustworthy, if eccentric, professional.

name: Quixote
title: Visionary
note: In the draft's opening, the tension of Nora's predicament with Deputy Director Marcus Leland is palpable, but there's a missed opportunity to elevate the stakes even more. Leland could confront Nora with more disturbing elements from the surviving hikers' reports or autopsy details, something not easily explained by the 'mass psychological event' label, forcing her to question the narrative they want her to believe. Additionally, during the parking lot scene with Danny, consider having him hint at mysterious government interest or interference, adding an extra layer of conspiracy to Nora's dilemma. This would deepen the mystery and raise the narrative stakes as Nora delves into the heart of the unknown with her geologist friend.
